title: Heleophylax lacustris
intro: Heleophylax lacustris is a type of aquatic plant, also known as lake helio, which is native to Western Australia and is characterized by its succulent leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Heleophylax lacustris include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Heleophylax lacustris include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
